name: Lago Caicedo from Salinas de Añana — ZEC Lago de Caicedo Yuso y Arreo, y Biotopo protegido del Diapiro de Añana
name_original: Lago Caicedo desde Salinas de Añana — ZEC Lago de Caicedo Yuso y Arreo, y Biotopo protegido del Diapiro de Añana
region_intro: Hiking around Añana, Spain, is characterized by its unique cultural landscape and diverse natural features. The region's centerpiece is the ancient Salt Valley, featuring thousands of terraced salt pans fed by hypersaline springs, creating a distinctive environment. Beyond the salt flats, the area includes surrounding hills, valleys, and the notable Lago de Caicedo, offering varied terrain for exploration. These landmarks and viewpoints provide a rich backdrop for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Añana The most popular hiking route is Lago Caicedo from Salinas de Añana — ZEC Lago de Caicedo Yuso y Arreo, y Biotopo protegido del Diapiro de Añana, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route leads hikers from Salinas de Añana to the natural attraction of Lago de Caicedo. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cantoblanco Loop from Salinas de Añana, a difficult 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain, providing a challenging experience through the region's varied terrain. Local hikers also love the View of the Añana Salt Flats loop from Añanako kuadrilla, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail leading through the unique Salt Valley, often completed in about 52 minutes. Hiking around Añana is defined by its ancient salt pans, surrounding hills, and the tranquil Lago de Caicedo. The network of trails offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging day hikes. The routes in Añana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Añana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Añana?
A: Añana offers a diverse network of nearly 100 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 18 easy, 57 moderate, and 15 difficult options to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Añana?
A: Hiking in Añana features varied terrain, from the distinctive terraced salt pans of the Valle Salado to surrounding hills and valleys. You'll encounter paths leading to natural attractions like Lago de Caicedo, offering a mix of cultural landscape and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks in Añana?
A: Yes, Añana has several easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10510310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Añana Salt Flats loop from Añanako kuadrilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 2.9 km trail through the unique Salt Valley, typically completed in under an hour. There are 18 easy routes in total.

Q: What are the best circular walks in Añana?
A: For a rewarding circular experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10510310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Añana Salt Flats loop from Añanako kuadrilla&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a concise exploration of the Salt Valley. Another option is the more comprehensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14994795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Caicedo – View of the Añana Salt Flats loop from Añanako kuadrilla&lt;/a&gt;, combining the salt flats with the tranquil lake.

Q: What interesting landmarks or viewpoints can I see while hiking in Añana?
A: Añana's hikes are rich with points of interest. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3413256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Añana&lt;/a&gt;, a unique man-made monument, or enjoy panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2905336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vitores&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caicedo Yuso (Lake Arreo)&lt;/a&gt; and various summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marinda&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montemayor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging day hikes in Añana?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous experience, Añana offers challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050830616/cantoblanco-loop-from-salinas-de-anana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantoblanco Loop from Salinas de Añana&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.3 km path with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding yet rewarding adventure through the region's varied terrain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Añana?
A: The komoot community highly rates Añana's trails, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique cultural landscape of the Salt Valley, the tranquility of Lago de Caicedo, and the diverse natural features that make each route distinct.

Q: Can I combine a visit to the Salt Valley with a hike to Lago de Caicedo?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Añana are designed to showcase both the ancient Salt Valley and the serene Lago de Caicedo. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050825412/lago-caicedo-from-salinas-de-anana-zec-lago-de-caicedo-yuso-y-arreo-y-biotopo-protegido-del-diapiro-de-anana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Caicedo from Salinas de Añana&lt;/a&gt; route, which takes you from the salt pans directly to the lake.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Añana?
A: Añana offers enjoyable hiking experiences throughout much of the year. The Salt Spa and Salt Workshop attractions are available between April and October, suggesting these months are particularly vibrant for visitors. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails and the unique salt landscape.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Añana is rich in history. Beyond the ancient Salt Valley itself, you can discover historical buildings within Salinas de Añana, such as the Church of Santa María de Villacones and the Palace of the Zambrana-Herrán family. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3939870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Valparaíso&lt;/a&gt; highlight also points to a significant historical site in the area.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Salt Valley?
A: Many trails provide stunning views of the Salt Valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10510310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Añana Salt Flats loop from Añanako kuadrilla&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed for this, offering an immersive experience of the gleaming white terraces. Longer routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20506531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Añana – View of the Añana Salt Flats loop from Añanako kuadrilla&lt;/a&gt; also incorporate these iconic vistas.
tips: The final touch of a beautiful walk from Salinas de Añana.

Legend has it that this lake arose as a result of a divine punishment. The inhabitants of the town that was under its waters denied bread three consecutive times to a beggar, who was none other than the Virgin. As punishment, he caused the waters to flood the town, which disappeared. In commemoration of that catastrophe, the hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Lago was built.
Salinas de Añana, for a few years in the process of recovery and exhibition. You can walk through them on a guided tour.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of the Lake
